name: Chestnut-And-Lentil Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 cup French green lentils (or Brown Lentils)
1 medium Carrot (diced into small, even squares)
1 large Garlic clove (peeled and finely chopped)
recipeInstructions:
FINISHING THE SOUP
3 tbsp Light olive oil
-Chestnuts (from above)
1 1/2 tsp Chopped fresh marjoram (or 1/2 tsp Dried Marjoram)
1/4 tsp Fennel seeds, crushed (or ground)
Fresh thyme sprigs (or 1 pinch Dried Thyme)
1/2 cup Dry white wine
1 tbsp Tomato paste
-Lentils (from above)
Water, stock or cream (as needed)
Salt
Freshly milled black pepper
Finely chopped parsley
Small croutons, fried in butter or oil (optional)
PREHEAT OVEN TO 350°F. Score chestnuts on their rounded sides and toss them in just enough oil to lightly coat them. Place chestnuts on a baking sheet. Bake until skins have opened and meat is tender, about 20 minutes. When cool enough to handle, peel and dice them into small pieces. Set aside. Rinse lentils and combine them in a soup pot with the water. Bring to boil. Cook a few minutes at gentle boil, removing foam that forms on surface. Add carrot, celery, onion, garlic, bay leaf, salt, and parsley. Lower heat and simmer until lentils are tender, about 35 minutes. When done, remove parsley sprigs and bay leaf. Correct seasoning and add salt, if necessary. Puree half the lentils until smooth, then recombine them with the rest.
In a large pan, warm olive oil over medium heat. Add roasted chestnuts and herbs. Cook, stirring frequently, for several minutes. Add wine and tomato paste. Stir to dissolve tomato paste and cook a few minutes to reduce wine. Stir in lentil mixture and simmer gently for 20 minutes. If soup is too thick, thin it with a little water, stock, or cream until of right consistency. Taste for salt and season with pepper. Serve with spoonful of olive oil swirled into each bowl. Sprinkle with parsley and croutons, if desired.
